goal: I want to become a web developer

matched_occupation.label: web developer
matched_occupation.description: Web developers develop, implement and document web-accessible software based on the designs provided. They align the client's web presence with its business strategy, troubleshoot software problems and issues and look for ways to improve the application.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Programming Fundamentals
objectives: Understand basic programming concepts like variables, data types, operators, and control flow.
Learn to write simple programs using a chosen programming language (e.g., Python, JavaScript).
Develop problem-solving skills through coding exercises.
Understand the concept of algorithms and their representation.
skills: Syntax of a chosen programming language

learning_path.title: Web Programming Basics: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
objectives: Learn the fundamentals of HTML for structuring web content.
Understand CSS for styling web pages and creating layouts.
Learn basic JavaScript for adding interactivity to websites.
Understand the DOM (Document Object Model) and how JavaScript interacts with it.

learning_path.title: Mastering Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
objectives: Learn to use an IDE (e.g., VS Code, IntelliJ IDEA, Eclipse) effectively.
Understand IDE features like code completion, debugging, and refactoring.
Configure IDE settings for optimal development workflow.
Learn to use IDE extensions and plugins to enhance functionality.

learning_path.title: Collaborative Web Development Platforms (Git & GitHub)
objectives: Understand the principles of version control using Git.
Learn to use GitHub for collaborative software development.
Master branching, merging, and pull requests.
Understand conflict resolution in Git.
skills: Git commands (commit, push, pull, branch, merge)

learning_path.title: Software Configuration Management (SCM) Tools
objectives: Understand the importance of SCM.
Learn to use a specific SCM tool (e.g., Git, SVN, Mercurial) in detail.
Understand branching strategies and release management.
Learn about continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD).

learning_path.title: Web Services: REST APIs
objectives: Understand the principles of RESTful web services.
Learn to design and implement REST APIs.
Understand HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE).
Learn to consume REST APIs using a programming language (e.g., JavaScript, Python).
prerequisites: Web Programming Basics: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript

learning_path.title: Using Application-Specific Interfaces (APIs)
objectives: Learn how to use a specific application's API (e.g., Google Maps API, Twitter API).
Understand API documentation and authentication.
Learn to integrate API functionality into your applications.
Understand rate limiting and error handling.

learning_path.title: Markup Languages: XML and JSON
objectives: Understand the purpose and structure of XML and JSON.
Learn to create and parse XML and JSON documents.
Understand the differences between XML and JSON.
Learn to use XML and JSON for data exchange.

learning_path.title: Using E-Services (e.g., Payment Gateways, Cloud Storage)
objectives: Understand the concept of e-services and their integration into applications.
Learn to use a specific e-service (e.g., Stripe, AWS S3).
Understand security considerations when using e-services.
Learn about API keys and authentication.

learning_path.title: Software Debugging Techniques
objectives: Learn effective debugging strategies.
Master debugging tools in your IDE.
Learn to identify and fix common programming errors.
Understand logging and error handling techniques.
